args_redacted.content: Zero trust security principles and defense in depth security are two different approaches to security. Zero trust security principles involve verifying the identity and permissions of all users and devices before granting access to a network or system. Defense in depth security, on the other hand, involves using multiple layers of security controls to protect against different types of threats. While both approaches have their own advantages and disadvantages, they can be used together to provide a more comprehensive security posture.
